ownReputation.title: Your Reputation
ownReputation.nameMismatchBody: We couldn’t match your business on Google Maps. This usually means the business name in your profile doesn’t match your Google listing exactly. Check your Settings and make sure the name matches your Google Business Profile precisely.

content.pageSubtitle: Walk through each section, copy what you need.
content.langWarning: You're viewing content in {lang}. Click Regenerate to switch to {target}.
content.genInProgress: Generating your content… this takes about 20 seconds.
content.noContentBody: Click Generate Content to create optimized descriptions, FAQs, schema markup, and social bios.
content.noProfile: Complete your business profile in Settings first.

description.whatItIs: Three description variants — one tuned for each platform you publish on.
description.whereToPaste: Website: paste in your homepage About section (and use as the page's meta description). Google: sign in to business.google.com → Edit profile → Business description. Yelp: biz.yelp.com → Edit Business Information → Description.
description.whyItMatters: Highest-impact AI-search content. Direct signal to ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overview. AI engines crawl your homepage + GBP + Yelp listings and cite the description text verbatim when answering "what does [business] do?" / "best [type] in [city]?" queries.

social.whatItIs: A short bio used consistently across every social platform you have.
social.whereToPaste: Instagram bio · Facebook Page About → Short Description · X (Twitter) bio · LinkedIn personal headline · TikTok bio · YouTube channel description (first line).
social.whyItMatters: Indirect but real citation source. Facebook + Instagram pages appear in Google search results and AI engines do cite them. Using the SAME bio across platforms creates Name + Address + Phone (NAP) consistency — a trust signal AI engines weigh when deciding whether your business is "real enough" to recommend. Inconsistent bios across platforms suggest a fly-by-night operation; consistent bios suggest legitimacy.

faq.whatItIs: 10 question-and-answer pairs written for the questions real customers ask AI engines.
faq.whereToPaste: Build a /faq page on your website (or add a FAQ section to your homepage / About page). Paste the Q&As as visible page content — the same way you would write any other article. No HTML or code involved at this step.
faq.whyItMatters: FAQs are the highest-leverage AI-search content per word. AI engines — especially ChatGPT and Perplexity — cite FAQ answers verbatim when responding to user questions that match. Verify each answer carefully — a wrong FAQ answer published on your site is worse than no FAQ at all. The next step (FAQ schema) is the technical wrapper that turns these Q&As into a Google rich-result snippet.

faq_schema.whatItIs: A JSON-LD wrapper around your FAQ Q&As that tells Google + AI engines exactly which question goes with which answer.
faq_schema.whereToPaste: Inside the head tag of your /faq page (the same page where you pasted the Q&As in step 3). The Copy button wraps it in a complete script[type=application/ld+json] tag — paste it as-is. If you don't maintain your own website code, forward this to your web administrator.
faq_schema.whyItMatters: This unlocks Google's expandable FAQ rich snippets — the boxes that appear directly in search results showing question-and-answer previews. Rich snippets dramatically increase click-through rate. AI engines also use the schema to understand the Q&A structure beyond just reading text. The schema and the visible Q&As work together: one without the other gets you partial benefit.

schema.whatItIs: Machine-readable description of your business in the format AI crawlers prefer.
schema.whereToPaste: Inside the head tag of your homepage (and any other key pages — About, Contact, Services). The Copy button wraps it in a complete script[type=application/ld+json] tag — paste it as-is.
schema.whyItMatters: Most direct signal you can give Google's Knowledge Graph + AI Overview. Required for rich-result eligibility (review stars, opening hours, price range showing in search results). Every AI crawler reads this — including GPTBot, PerplexityBot, ClaudeBot. A single correct JSON-LD block is worth more than 1000 words of marketing copy for AI search.

website.label: Website
website.hint: 300–400 words for your homepage / About page

faqSeeds.title: Questions you actually hear (optional)
faqSeeds.hint: Paste questions you hear from real customers, one per line. We'll use them verbatim as the first questions in your FAQ and generate the rest. Up to 10. Saved with your content; you only have to type these once.

existingFaqs.title: Existing FAQs from your website (optional)
existingFaqs.hint: Already have an FAQ page? Paste it here. We'll use your existing Q&As verbatim and generate fresh AI-search-optimized questions on different topics — combined into one valid FAQ schema.
existingFaqs.format: Format: question on one line, answer on the next, blank line between pairs.
existingFaqs.detected: Detected {count} Q&A {pairWord} · total final FAQ count: {total}

faqContent.title: FAQ Content
faqContent.subtitle: {count} Q&As — paste these on a /faq page

faqSchema.title: FAQ Schema (JSON-LD)
faqSchema.subtitle: Paste inside the head tag of your /faq page.
faqSchema.emptyBody: The FAQ schema is generated automatically from your FAQ Q&As. Go back to step 3 to verify your FAQs, or click Regenerate to create new ones.

schema.title: Schema Markup (JSON-LD)
schema.subtitle: Paste inside the head tag of your homepage.
schema.profileWarningTitle: Complete your profile to qualify for Google rich results

content.validationWarning: Note: {warnings}. Re-run if anything looks off.

schemaWarning.title: ⚠ Heads up — this part requires editing your website's HTML
schemaWarning.body: If you're not comfortable editing code, that's completely fine. Copy this and forward it to your web administrator or developer with a single line: "Please paste this inside the head tag of our homepage." That's all the context they need — they'll know what to do.
schemaWarning.diyTitle: Doing it yourself? Quick guide for common website platforms
schemaWarning.wordpress: WordPress: install the free Insert Headers and Footers plugin → Settings → Insert Headers and Footers → Scripts in Header → paste. (If Yoast SEO or Rank Math is already installed, they have schema settings too.)
schemaWarning.squarespace: Squarespace: Settings → Advanced → Code Injection → Header → paste.
schemaWarning.wix: Wix: Site Dashboard → Settings → Custom Code → Add Custom Code → choose Head placement → paste.
schemaWarning.shopify: Shopify: Online Store → Themes → Edit Code → open theme.liquid → paste just before </head>.
schemaWarning.webflow: Webflow: Project Settings → Custom Code → Head Code → paste.
schemaWarning.custom: Custom-built site: open the HTML template that includes the head tag (often index.html or a layout file) and paste this anywhere between the opening and closing head tags. Order doesn't matter.
schemaWarning.caution: When in doubt, save this as a draft and ask. Pasting the wrong code in the wrong place can occasionally break a page's layout — your web admin can do this in 2 minutes.

edit.regenFailed: Regenerate failed — try again or simplify your notes.
edit.regenNotesTitle: What should change? (optional — leave blank for a fresh take)
edit.regenNotesPlaceholder: e.g. make it shorter; remove the part about parking; mention free consultations
edit.faqRegenTitle: What should change about this Q&A?
edit.faqRegenPlaceholder: e.g. The answer is wrong about pricing
edit.noContent: No content for this section yet.

citations.title: Directory Presence
citations.subtitle: Where you and your competitors are listed across major directories. Each directory listing is a citation that AI engines weigh as a trust signal.
citations.gapsTitle: Gaps — competitors are listed here, you are not
citations.noGaps: No directory gaps detected — you appear wherever your competitors do.
citations.noData: We didn't detect any directory listings for you or your competitors in this audit's search results. This is normal for very local or very new businesses.
citations.footer: Each directory listing strengthens your AI search visibility — competitors cited on more directories are more likely to be cited by ChatGPT and Perplexity.

empty.profileBody: We need your business name, type, and city before we can find competitors.
empty.noAuditBody: Once you run your first AI visibility audit, we'll automatically identify your top 3 local competitors.
empty.noCompetitorsTitle: No local competitors found in Google's map results
empty.noCompetitorsBody: We couldn't find businesses Google associates with your category in your city. Two common reasons:
empty.thinMarketTitle: 1. You're in a thin local market
empty.thinMarketBody: Few similar businesses are indexed near you. This is genuinely an opportunity — rank locally now, before competitors arrive. Re-run the audit in a week to check again.
empty.notLocalTitle: 2. Your business isn't local in nature
empty.notLocalBody: SaaS, software, online services, consulting — these don't compete locally. Your real competitors are national or global, not the businesses next door. We're building industry-wide competitor analysis for these cases (planned for a future release). For now, the audit's GBP / Reviews / Website / AI Citation pillars still apply — just without the comparative table.
